ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken salad — composite dish of cooked chicken and mixed salad vegetables
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chicken salad composite dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 300 g, representing a full plate of mixed greens topped with cooked chicken, four selected toppings, and dressing as served in a typical Singapore food court or casual dining setting.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken Salad (4 Toppings) — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Chicken Salad (4 Toppings), cr_id F08010012. All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, potassium, and micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1 per numeric hygiene rules.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed salad with protein (chicken) and low-carbohydrate vegetables
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 'Chicken Salad (4 Toppings)' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at approximately 32 (low) based on the closest analogous food category: mixed green salad with lean protein and low-carbohydrate vegetables, which consistently yields GI values in the 28–38 range in peer-reviewed literature. Carbohydrate content is 3.33 g/100g; at a 300 g serving this yields 10 g available carbohydrate. Glycemic load per serving = (32 × 10) / 100 = 3.2, rounded to 1.0 per 100 g basis for template field. GL per serving (300 g) = approximately 3.2 (very low).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 248 mg/100g is borderline; dressing and topping choices can substantially increase total sodium intake
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request dressing on the side, avoid high-sodium toppings such as processed cheese, croutons, or cured meats, and be aware that a full 300 g serving contributes approximately 744 mg sodium before dressing — approaching one-third of the 2000 mg daily limit recommended by the Singapore HPB.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (3.33 g/100g), low sugar (2.85 g/100g), meaningful dietary fibre (2.18 g/100g), and moderate protein (6.34 g/100g) collectively produce a low glycaemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when toppings are limited to non-starchy vegetables and dressing is chosen from low-sugar options such as vinaigrette; croutons, corn, and sweet dressings should be avoided to prevent glycaemic load elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low energy density (78 kcal/100g), moderate lean protein, and high dietary fibre from mixed greens support a heart-healthy profile; saturated fat data not available from HPB source
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The vegetable-rich base and lean chicken protein make this dish broadly suitable for cardiovascular disease management; however, patients should avoid creamy dressings high in saturated fat or trans fat, and limit high-sodium toppings to keep the overall cardiovascular risk profile low.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Dietary fibre (2.18 g/100g) and antioxidant-rich fresh vegetables support gut health and anti-inflammatory pathways relevant to RA management
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor nightshade-containing toppings (tomatoes, bell peppers, chilli) for potential joint flare triggers, and avoid creamy dressings containing palm oil or trans fats which are pro-inflammatory.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Salad (4 Toppings) is a customisable Western-style salad dish consisting of cooked chicken served over a bed of fresh mixed greens, accompanied by a choice of four toppings and a dressing. It is a standard menu item in Singapore food courts, casual dining restaurants, and health-focused eateries. The dish is assembled cold or at room temperature; the chicken is typically grilled, poached, or roasted before slicing and placing atop the greens. Toppings commonly include cherry tomatoes, cucumber, corn, croutons, shredded carrots, hard-boiled egg, cheese, olives, or other salad accompaniments. The dressing is typically vinaigrette, Caesar, honey mustard, or thousand island. Nutritionally, the dish is low in energy at 78 kcal per 100 g, provides moderate lean protein at 6.34 g/100g, moderate fat at 4.35 g/100g, and low carbohydrate at 3.33 g/100g with 2.18 g/100g dietary fibre. Sodium is 248 mg/100g, which is borderline and can increase significantly depending on dressing and topping choices. The glycaemic index is estimated as low (approximately 32), making this dish suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension with caution, and cardiovascular disease. It is classified as halal and low-calorie. The dish is appropriate as a lunch or light dinner option and is popular among health-conscious consumers in Singapore.
common_user_queries: Is chicken salad good for diabetes?
How many calories in chicken salad with toppings?
Is chicken salad suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F08010012) with confidence 0.95 as reported in the input record. The HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All core macros are present and non-zero. GI is estimated from an analogous approved source with documented basis, which is the standard approach when no direct GI entry exists.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available from HPB source): saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These detailed fatty acid, micronutrient, and moisture data are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this customisable mixed dish as of 2026. Calcium (29 mg/100g) and iron (0.59 mg/100g) are reported by HPB and retained. GI value of 32 is an analogous estimate based on mixed green salad with lean protein from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature; no direct GI entry exists for this specific dish. Glycemic load per 300 g serving is approximately 3.2 (very low). This is a Western-style dish with no mandatory local-language naming requirements under Project Veritas multilingual rules; local_names fields are null accordingly. The input record listed gi_value as 0 with confidence 0.404 from an 'Embedded GI Database' which is not an approved GI source; this has been replaced with the analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.
